reasoning: The State of Emergency was declared on March 31, 2025, to run for 30 days, ending exactly on April 29, 2025. It was enacted due to severe power outages on Upolu island. Temporary generators from New Zealand arrived on April 5, and as of April 21, the Electric Power Corporation officially cancelled all power rationing, confirming that the generation capacity is now sufficient to meet demand. Because the immediate crisis has been resolved and rationing ended, it is highly unlikely the government will need to extend the State of Emergency. Therefore, the probability that it will still be in effect on April 30, 2025, after its scheduled expiration, is very low.
